data.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge-canary.yml` runs (cron hourly). Its "Install rebar" step runs `pip install .` — plain rebar, NO `[agents]` extra, and no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` in the job env.
2. When the reconciler is healthy (`stale=false`) and an open `heartbeat-alert` ticket exists, the "Close alert ticket on recovery" step runs `rebar transition <id> open closed --reason "Fixed: ..."` under `set -euo pipefail`.
3. That close triggers the completion-verification close gate (`verify.require_completion_verification_for_close=true`), which requires the `[agents]` extra + a model API key.

## Expected vs Actual

- Expected: the canary closes its own recovery/alert ticket and the job stays green; the heartbeat self-heals.
- Actual: the gate cannot run (no [agents]/key) -> fail-closed -> `rebar transition` exits non-zero -> the step (under set -e) fails -> canary job RED on every recovery, and the alert ticket (aa7b / luxe-adder-faith, P1) is stuck OPEN. The same defect affects the "Close binding-drift alert on recovery" step.
- A permanently-red canary defeats staleness alerting: a real outage becomes indistinguishable from the constant red.

## Root Cause

The canary manages bot-generated alert tickets, which are not work tickets with verifiable acceptance criteria; subjecting their auto-close to the completion-verification gate is wrong. The install also omits [agents].

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The canary recovery-close steps (heartbeat + binding-drift) close their alert tickets without tripping the completion-verification gate (e.g. `--force-close="Fixed: ..."`).
- [ ] The canary job is green when the reconciler is healthy and an alert ticket is being closed.
- [ ] Verified (dry-run / reasoning) that the recovery-close path no longer invokes the LLM gate.
- [ ] luxe-adder-faith (aa7b) is closed and does not recur on the next healthy cycle.
data.title: reconcile-bridge-canary: recovery-close trips completion-verification gate -> canary red + alert tickets stuck open
